active_paradigms.idea: Use scipy.optimize.minimize with trust-constr method for constrained position optimization
active_paradigms.description: Apply scipy.optimize.minimize with method='trust-constr' to optimize circle positions (52 variables for 26 centers). Compute radii analytically: for each circle i, r_i = min(x_i, y_i, 1-x_i, 1-y_i, min(d_ij/2 for all j≠i)). This automatically satisfies all constraints. The objective is to minimize -sum(radii). Use scipy.optimize.Bounds([0]*52, [1]*52) for positions. trust-constr uses a trust-region approach specifically designed for nonlinear constrained optimization, handling the non-smooth objective (due to min operations) better than SLSQP. Initialize from hexagonal pattern (5-4-5-4-5-3 rows) with optimized spacing.
active_paradigms.cautions: The min operations create non-smooth regions where gradients are undefined. trust-constr handles this better than SLSQP but may have convergence issues. Use multiple random starting points around the hexagonal initialization. Consider setting gtol=1e-8 and maxiter=1000. The objective is highly non-convex with many local minima - may need 5-10 restarts.

active_paradigms.idea: Use scipy.optimize.basinhopping for stochastic global optimization
active_paradigms.description: Apply scipy.optimize.basinhopping to globally optimize circle positions. Define objective as -sum(radii) with analytical radius computation. Use L-BFGS-B as local minimizer with bounds [0,1] on all 52 position variables. Set niter=300 and temperature=0.3 for good exploration-exploitation balance. basinhopping performs random perturbations followed by local minimization, allowing escape from local optima that trap SLSQP. This stochastic global approach is fundamentally different from deterministic Voronoi relaxation and can discover better packings by exploring multiple basins.
active_paradigms.cautions: Tune temperature (0.1-1.0) and stepsize (0.05-0.2) - too high wastes computation, too low prevents escape from local optima. Use take_step callback for problem-specific perturbations that respect bounds. The local minimizer may struggle at min boundaries - consider Nelder-Mead as alternative. Start from hexagonal initialization. Set niter_success=50 for early stopping.

active_paradigms.idea: Use scipy.optimize.shgo for deterministic global optimization with homology
active_paradigms.description: Apply scipy.optimize.shgo (Simplicial Homology Global Optimization) for systematic global optimization. SHGO builds a simplicial complex to explore the search space deterministically, identifying basins of attraction through topological analysis. Define bounds [0,1]^52 for positions. Use analytical radius computation for objective. Set options={'n': 128, 'iters': 7, 'sampling_method': 'sobol'} for thorough exploration with quasi-random sampling. SHGO can guarantee finding the global optimum given sufficient iterations, making it ideal for non-convex packing problems.
active_paradigms.cautions: SHGO has O(n^d) complexity - computationally expensive for 52 dimensions. Use minimizer_kwargs={'method': 'Nelder-Mead'} for non-smooth objectives. Consider reducing dimension by fixing corner circles or using symmetry constraints. The sampling_method 'sobol' provides better coverage than 'simplicial' for high dimensions. Set iters lower (3-5) initially to check progress before increasing.

tried_paradigms.idea: Use scipy.optimize.minimize with SLSQP to jointly optimize all 78 variables (26 centers × 2 coords + 26 radii) with explicit constraints
tried_paradigms.description: Formulate as a continuous optimization problem: maximize sum(radii) subject to: (1) no overlaps: ||c_i - c_j|| >= r_i + r_j for all pairs, (2) boundary constraints: r_i <= x_i, r_i <= y_i, r_i <= 1-x_i, r_i <= 1-y_i. Use SLSQP method with constraint Jacobians for efficiency. Initialize with hexagonal-like pattern: place circles in rows with alternating offsets. Use multiple random perturbations of initial positions to escape local optima.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: sum of all 26 radii (primary objective), constraint satisfaction
tried_paradigms.cautions: SLSQP may converge to local optima - use 10+ random initializations. Constraints must be formulated as inequalities (g(x) >= 0). The problem has 78 variables and ~351 pairwise overlap constraints plus 104 boundary constraints. Scale variables appropriately.

tried_paradigms.idea: Use scipy.optimize.differential_evolution for global optimization of circle positions with radii computed analytically
tried_paradigms.description: Optimize only the 52 position variables (centers), computing maximum possible radii analytically for each position configuration. The objective function: for given centers, compute r_i = min(distance to walls, distance to other circles/2) then return sum(r_i). Differential evolution is population-based and handles non-convex landscapes well. Use bounds [0.05, 0.95] for all coordinates. Use strategy 'best1bin' with population size 15-20, max iterations 1000.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: sum of radii computed from positions, global exploration of position space
tried_paradigms.cautions: DE is slower but finds better global optima. The objective function has many local minima due to discrete-like structure of optimal packings. Set polish=True for local refinement. Use workers=-1 for parallelization if available. Seed with hexagonal lattice points.

tried_paradigms.idea: Use scipy.spatial.Voronoi diagram to guide iterative circle placement with radius maximization
tried_paradigms.description: Start with an initial packing (e.g., hexagonal pattern). Iteratively: (1) Compute Voronoi diagram of current circle centers, (2) For each cell, the maximum inscribed circle gives an upper bound on possible radius, (3) Use centroid or incenter of each Voronoi cell as new position, (4) Compute actual radii respecting overlaps. Repeat until convergence. This exploits the geometric fact that optimal packings have circles centered in their Voronoi cells. Initialize with 5-4-5-4-5-3 row pattern (26 circles) following hexagonal packing density.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: positions guided by Voronoi cell geometry, natural spacing between circles
tried_paradigms.cautions: Voronoi-based iteration may not converge - limit to 50 iterations. Handle boundary cells specially (clip to unit square). The Voronoi approach works best as a heuristic to find good initial positions, which can then be refined. Some cells may be unbounded near edges.

tried_paradigms.idea: Direct SLSQP optimization of all 78 variables (26 centers + 26 radii) with nonlinear constraints
tried_paradigms.description: Use scipy.optimize.minimize with SLSQP to directly maximize sum of radii. Variables are [x1,y1,r1, x2,y2,r2, ..., x26,y26,r26] (78 total). Constraints: (1) boundary constraints: r_i ≤ x_i ≤ 1-r_i and r_i ≤ y_i ≤ 1-r_i for each circle, (2) non-overlap: ||(xi,yi)-(xj,yj)||² ≥ (ri+rj)² for all pairs. Use multiple random initializations with hexagonal-informed starting points. The objective is simply -sum(radii) (negative for minimization).
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Directly maximize sum_radii by optimizing all positions and radii jointly
tried_paradigms.cautions: SLSQP needs feasible initial point. Start with small radii (0.05-0.08) and valid positions. Use inequality constraints in list format. Run from multiple initializations to avoid local minima. Constraint tolerance of 1e-7 recommended.

tried_paradigms.idea: Trust-region constrained optimization (trust-constr) with Jacobian/Hessian sparsity for efficiency
tried_paradigms.description: Use scipy.optimize.minimize with method='trust-constr' which handles large numbers of nonlinear constraints efficiently. Define constraints as scipy.optimize.NonlinearConstraint with sparse Jacobian. The 325 pairwise non-overlap constraints have sparse structure - each constraint involves only 6 variables (two circles). Use SequentialQuadraticProgramming-like approach built into trust-constr. Initialize with circles placed in a perturbed hexagonal grid with small initial radii, then let optimizer grow them.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: sum_radii with efficient handling of 325+ nonlinear constraints
tried_paradigms.cautions: trust-constr requires bounds and constraints in specific format. Use Bounds object for variable bounds. Provide constraint Jacobian for speed. May need 10000+ iterations. Start with tight tolerance on constraints.

tried_paradigms.idea: Force-directed placement with radius growth simulation
tried_paradigms.description: Implement a physics-based approach: treat circles as expanding bubbles with repulsive forces between overlapping circles and from boundaries. Iteratively: (1) compute repulsive forces based on overlap amounts, (2) move centers proportionally to forces, (3) grow all radii by small amount if feasible, else shrink. Use numpy for vectorized force computation. This mimics how actual circle packing would settle naturally. Run for thousands of iterations with decreasing step sizes. The key is simultaneous radius growth with position adjustment.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: sum_radii through natural physics-based expansion and collision avoidance
tried_paradigms.cautions: Need careful tuning of force constants and step sizes. Must clip positions to maintain boundary feasibility. Use adaptive step size that decreases over iterations. Check feasibility after each iteration.
